name: secmo
killReason: Two-strikes retirement. v1 (top-3, 3-month) failed pre-commit: Sharpe 0.59, DD 40.7%. v2 (top-1, 6-month, canonical Antonacci variant) also failed: Sharpe 0.51, DD 35.4%. Both versions cleared PF/CAGR/Z/N but consistently underperformed on Sharpe and drawdown. In-sample Sharpe 0.36 vs holdout 0.74 on v2 suggests higher regime-dependence than CATF. Sector momentum at this retail scale may not be viable without additional risk-management (vol-targeting, tighter absolute filter). Not pursued further.
thesis: Within the US equity market, sectors rotate predictably as economic cycles shift — tech and discretionary lead early recovery, energy and materials lead late cycle, utilities and staples lead in downturns. Cross-sectional momentum (rank sectors by recent return, concentrate in the leader) captures these rotations. Published canonical variant is Antonacci's top-1 with 6-month lookback. Edge source is different from CATF's absolute cross-asset trend: this is relative strength within one asset class, CATF is time-series momentum across asset classes.

revisions.change: v1 → v2: topN 3 → 1, lookbackBars 63 → 126, maxSinglePositionPct 0.40 → 1.0. Expected CAGR/Sharpe/DD ranges shifted upward to reflect the top-1 concentrated variant.
revisions.reason: v1 (top-3, 3-month) failed pre-commit backtest on 2008-2024 data: Sharpe 0.59 (need 0.7), Max DD 40.7% (kill 35%). The rules I picked weren't the canonical published variant. Antonacci's documented top-1 / 6-month variant is simpler and has stronger published results (Sharpe 0.8-1.0, CAGR 12-16%). v2 is a *strategy redesign* based on literature, not a criteria relaxation — kill-switch DD stays at 35% and v2 must pass on the same bar v1 failed. Two-strikes rule: if v2 also fails, SECMO is retired entirely.
